subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear and appropriate purpose and focus, as evidenced by the concise, engaging title and contextual definitions.
The research question is clearly stated, sharply focused on the correlational relationship, and consistently connected to the analysis throughout the essay.
The methodology demonstrates a mostly complete, informed selection of relevant secondary sources, including empirical studies, a meta-analysis, and a theoretical model.
weaknesses: Occasional drift between causation and correlation introduces slight ambiguity in the stated focus.
The demographic bounds of the research question are not explicitly defined, which weakens the precision of the question.
The absence of explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria and a detailed search strategy limits methodological transparency.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently communicates the topic with clarity and focus (two strands at band 5), and formulates a sharply focused research question linked throughout the essay (band 5). The methodological approach is generally appropriate and informed (upper band 3-4), but lacks explicit procedural detail and primary data, preventing placement in the top band. On balance, criterion A performance aligns with a score of 5 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent subject knowledge, accurately explaining NPD criteria, distinctions between grandiose and vulnerable narcissism, and integrating the SPIN model coherently.
Consistently relevant and precise application of peer-reviewed sources, including correct reporting of sample sizes, instruments, and reliability statistics.
Accurate and consistent use of discipline-specific terminology (e.g., cross-sectional, longitudinal, Likert scale, internal consistency) throughout the essay.
weaknesses: Minor conflation of addiction terminology with formal clinical disorders appears in places, though it does not undermine overall coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits excellent command of psychological concepts, applies sources with coherence and relevance, and uses terminology accurately and consistently. The minor conflation noted is trivial relative to the overall depth and precision. This performance merits the top mark of 6 out of 6 for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: The student conducts excellent, consistently relevant research, balancing supporting and counter evidence and linking findings to theoretical frameworks.
Analysis is effective, with well-supported conclusions about sample biases, design limitations, and bidirectional ambiguity.
Discussion presents a well-structured argument with critical evaluation of methodological strengths and weaknesses, and thoughtful recommendations for future research.
weaknesses: Statistical effect sizes and significance values are not reported, reducing analytical transparency.
Causal language occasionally slips into correlational discussion, which could mislead interpretation.
Alternative theoretical frameworks beyond the SPIN model are not explored, limiting the breadth of critical evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates high-level critical thinking: research selection is consistently relevant (band 10-12), analysis is effective and conclusions well supported (band 10-12), and discussion is well structured with critical evaluation (band 7-9). Omissions in statistical reporting and theoretical breadth prevent the very top mark, placing the performance firmly at 10 out of 12.

strengths: All required structural elements are present and correctly ordered (title page, table of contents, headings, pagination), guiding the reader effectively.
Citations and reference list follow APA conventions, and section headings clearly demarcate the transition into the evaluative Discussion.
weaknesses: Inconsistent styling in the table of contents (mixed bolding and dot leaders) affects navigational clarity.
The figure caption is duplicated and the embedded diagram appears as a code block, disrupting visual flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets all IB structural requirements with coherent layout and formatting (band 3-4). Minor inconsistencies in styling and figure presentation are present but do not impede overall readability, justifying the top mark of 4 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
Psychology terminology such as âcross-sectional,â âlongitudinal,â âLikert scale,â âinternal consistency,â âProblematic Internet Use,â and precise DSM-5 language is deployed **accurately and consistently**. Concept labels (grandiose vs vulnerable narcissism, self-promotion, status pursuit) are used correctly across sections without drift.  

The studentâs prose occasionally contains minor stylistic repetition, yet conceptual vocabulary remains precise and appropriate to an EE in Psychology, demonstrating command of disciplinary lexicon. This aligns with the descriptors for the highest band, warranting a score 5.

The layout demonstrates attention to formatting: a functional table of contents, bolded headings, figure caption, and APA reference list. Word count, title and page numbers are supplied. Citations largely follow APA conventions.  

Some minor inconsistencies occur (mix of bold and italic styling, occasional spacing errors, and a duplicated figure caption). These small issues mean layout is not flawlessly executed, but most considerations are correctly applied, matching the upper band (3-4) at score 3.

Throughout the essay the student demonstrates **strong subject knowledge**: definitions of NPD, distinctions between grandiose and vulnerable narcissism, explanation of DSM-5 criteria, and application of the SPIN model all reveal conceptual depth.  

Empirical studies are summarised accurately with key details (sample size, instruments, statistics such as Cronbachâs Î±). The student integrates theory (sociocultural approach, status-pursuit framework) with research findings to craft coherent arguments, showing understanding of how personality constructs interface with social-media environments.  

Minor inaccuracies (e.g., slight conflation of addiction terminology with formal disorders) are present but do not undermine the overall excellence. Consequently, the work fits securely in the 5-6 band (score 5).

The student adopts a literature-review methodology, selecting peer-reviewed empirical studies, a meta-analysis and a theoretical model (SPIN). Source choices are generally appropriate, recent and relevant, showing informed selection (e.g., inclusion of both supporting and counter evidence, longitudinal vs cross-sectional designs).  

However, the essay remains wholly secondary; no primary data collection, operationalisation of variables or explicit criteria for source inclusion/exclusion are provided. Discussion of methodological limitations (e.g., self-report bias, cross-sectional constraints) is present but not systematically linked to an articulated search strategy or data-handling procedure.  

Thus the methodology is âmostly completeâ with a âgenerally relevantâ range of sources, justifying the 3-4 band (upper end, score 4) rather than the top band that would require a fully explicit, rigorously justified research method.

The bibliography and in-text discussion draw on a **good spread of contemporary, peer-reviewed sources** (2017-2020 studies, DSM-5, a 2019 process model, a 2020 systematic review). Each source is clearly relevant to the research question, and the student purposefully balances supporting and counter evidence.  

While coverage is not exhaustiveâonly a handful of key studies are examinedâand explicit inclusion/exclusion criteria are absent, the research selected is consistently aligned to the question of narcissistic traits and SMU. Minor reliance on convenience samples within those studies is acknowledged but not fully offset. The overall quality and relevance correspond to the 7-9 band, positioned at score 8 to reflect solid but not exemplary breadth.

The research question â âTo what extent are symptoms associated with Narcissistic Personality Disorder correlated to social media use?â â is sharply articulated, framed as a correlational enquiry and consistently referenced across introduction, body, discussion and conclusion.  

All major sections return to this question, for example when evaluating Andreassen et al. (2017) and Reed et al. (2018) or when contrasting supporting and counter evidence in the Discussion. The student also justifies the importance of resolving bidirectional ambiguity, reinforcing the focus throughout.  

Some scope creep (occasional mention of âinfluenceâ or âonsetâ implying causation) prevents a perfect score, yet the question remains clearly stated, well-focused and tightly connected to the subsequent analysis, meriting high band 5-6 (score 5).

The essay follows IB conventions: title page with word count, abstract-style introduction, logical sectioning (Introduction, Body, Discussion, Conclusion), numbered pages, table of contents and reference list in APA-style layout. Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively and the overall flow is coherent.  

Because every required structural element is present and appropriately ordered, the work clearly meets the âclearly appropriateâ descriptor. Minor pagination inconsistencies in the extract do not detract from overall compliance, justifying the top band (score 4).

The Discussion section brings together findings, contrasts divergent results, and evaluates methodological weaknesses, showing a **reasoned, partially critical argument**. The student identifies the dominance of cross-sectional designs, the role of platform type, and addresses alternative interpretations.  

Evaluation is structured and linked back to the research question; however, it stops short of a fully critical appraisalâethical considerations, cultural bias and publication bias receive limited attention. The concluding reflections recommend longitudinal designs but could articulate more specific future research pathways.  

Overall, the argument is well-structured with definite critical engagement, aligning with the 7-9 band at score 7.

Analysis extends beyond summary: the student critiques sampling bias, cross-sectional limitations, gender imbalance and self-report issues, and links empirical patterns back to the SPIN model and sociocultural theory. Conclusions about bidirectional ambiguity are logically derived from evidence.  

Nonetheless, depth is unevenâstatistical effect sizes are rarely interrogated, and causal language occasionally slips into correlational arguments. Synthesis is competent but could probe alternative explanations (e.g., personality comorbidity) more rigorously.  

These features represent **clear, mostly supported analysis** within the 7-9 band, meriting score 7 rather than the very top.

annotations.comment: The term âDSM-5â appears in italics; DSM titles are protocol names and should not be italicizedâadjust to plain text per APA style.
The term âbidirectional relationshipâ is introduced without clear definition; explicitly define bidirectionality to orient the reader to the scope of the correlational analysis.
The figure caption appears twice; remove duplication and integrate the model as a formal figure caption with consistent styling.
The diagram is embedded as a code block (<pre>), disrupting visual flow; convert it into a labeled figure graphic to align with IB presentation standards.
An abstract is required for IB Extended Essays to summarise aims, methods, and conclusions succinctly; include a 300-word abstract immediately following the title page.
The table of contents employs mixed bold styling inconsistently across entries; standardize formatting for headings and dot leaders to improve navigational clarity.
Numbers are inconsistently styled (e.g., âseventy-four participantsâ); use numerals for sample sizes to conform with academic conventions.
The acknowledgement of self-report bias is appropriate, but further detail on mitigation strategies (e.g., triangulating survey data with behavioral logs) would enhance methodological critique.
The transition to the Discussion section is clearly indicated by the heading, effectively guiding the reader to the evaluative portion of the essay.
The title effectively signals the core variables (social media use and NPD traits) and aligns with IB conventions, guiding the reader immediately to the psychological focus and purpose.
The research question is clearly stated but omits the demographic bounds (age range) referenced in the introduction; specify the target population to sharpen focus and avoid ambiguity.
The student demonstrates excellent integration of subject-specific terminology and theoretical framing, clearly distinguishing clinical NPD criteria from broader trait narcissism.
This paragraph contains very long sentences that could be broken into shorter statements to improve readability and maintain reader engagement.
The systematic search strategy is commendable, but the student should specify inclusion/exclusion criteria (e.g., publication years, languages) to demonstrate rigor in source selection.
The Conclusion restates findings but would be strengthened by outlining explicit future research designs (e.g., longitudinal mixed-methods) to directly address the bidirectional ambiguity.
A sentence is cut off mid-thought, indicating a drafting error; ensure the text is complete to maintain coherence and professional presentation.
The use of the AXIS tool is appropriate; however, report inter-rater reliability or consensus methods to validate quality assessments in the systematic review.
While the SPIN model is well explained, the student should clarify how this framework informed the selection and analysis of empirical studies to strengthen methodological transparency.
The conclusion aptly summarises limitations but should propose specific measures (e.g., validated behavioral analytics) to overcome self-report biases in future studies.
The subsection âa) Supporting Evidenceâ effectively signposts the beginning of empirical analysis, aiding reader navigation through the structure.
Mathematical notations use HTML <math> tags; to adhere to academic conventions, present reliability coefficients using LaTeX (e.g., $$\alpha=0.88$$).

The strengths and limitations of Andreassen et al. are described, but the student should explicitly link these methodological considerations back to the research question on bidirectional correlation.
The study description is comprehensive but could be made more reader-friendly by summarizing instrument details in a table rather than lengthy prose.
When reporting significant relationships, include correlation coefficients (e.g., r-values) and p-values to strengthen analytic transparency.

The absence of significant correlations in Frederick and Zhang (2019) contradicts prior studies; the student should analyze potential reasons for this divergenceâsuch as sample demographics or variable operationalization.
While synthesising findings with the SPIN model, the student could deepen critical evaluation by contrasting alternative theoretical frameworks to broaden the discussion.
Reference section lacks hanging indents and consistent italicization; apply APA formatting uniformly across all entries.
The DOI links are inline rather than presented as active URLs per APA; ensure DOIs are formatted as full URLs and remove unnecessary retrieval statements.
